introductionSomali: Dib u eeg dhammaan waxyaabihii aad ku baratay heerka A2, ogow meelaha aad ku fiican tahay iyo casharrada aad u baahan tahay inaad ku celceliso kahor imtixaanka kama dambaysta ah.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Past Simple, regular and irregular verbs, was/were, did/did not, past-time expressions, sequencing and narratives. Qaybtani waxay muujisaa strengths, weak areas iyo meesha dib loogu noqdo.
learningObjectives: Dib u eeg Past Events and Experiences.
Aqoonso jawaabta saxda ah iyo sababteeda.
Calaamadee meelaha xooggan iyo meelaha tabarta yar.
Adeegso recommended section IDs marka practice dheeraad ahi loo baahan yahay.

grammar.titleEnglish: Past Events and Experiences language review
grammar.explanationSomali: Past Simple, regular and irregular verbs, was/were, did/did not, past-time expressions, sequencing and narratives. Xaaladda iyo macnaha ayaa go’aaminaya tense-ka, modal-ka, connector-ka ama communication function-ka.
grammar.rule: Past Simple, regular and irregular verbs, was/were, did/did not, past-time expressions, sequencing and narratives.
grammar.sentenceStructure: context + accurate A2 form + useful detail + appropriate response

positiveExamples.english: I have already checked the fictional details.
positiveExamples.somali: Hore ayaan u hubiyay faahfaahinta tusaalaha ah.

positiveExamples.english: Could you tell me where the office is?

positiveExamples.english: If the time changes, I will send an update.

positiveExamples.english: Although the route is longer, it is more convenient.

positiveExamples.somali: Waxaan jeclaan lahaa beddel, fadlan.

positiveExamples.english: They are meeting the adviser tomorrow.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxay la kulmayaan lataliyaha berri.

positiveExamples.somali: Waa inaad dib u eegtaa qaybta lagu taliyay.

negativeExamples.english: The learner did not use real personal information.

commonMistakesSomali: Ka dib did isticmaal base verb.
Present Perfect ha la raacin waqti hore oo dhammaaday.
Indirect question-ka ku hay statement word order.
Kala saar must not iyo do not have to.

dialogues.titleSomali: A2 Unit 1 review dialogue • listening-style • TTS-ready

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Could you explain the situation clearly?
lines.somali: Xaaladda si cad ma u sharxi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Yes. I checked the details before choosing a response.
lines.somali: Haa. Faahfaahinta ayaan hubiyay ka hor inta aanan jawaab dooran.

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What happened next?
lines.somali: Maxaa xigay?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I used the A2 language from this review section.
lines.somali: Waxaan adeegsaday luqadda A2 ee qaybtan dib-u-eegista.

practiceExercises.question: “yesterday” maxay ka dhigan tahay?
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabtu waa macnaha saxda ah ee yesterday. Review source: A2 Unit 1.

practiceExercises.question: Turjun: I worked yesterday.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Turjumaaddani waxay gudbinaysaa dhacdo hore. Review source: A2 Unit 1.

practiceExercises.question: Dooro jumladda Past Simple ee saxda ah.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jumladda saxda ahi waxay isticmaashaa qaabka past-ka si habboon. Review source: A2 Unit 1.

practiceExercises.question: She ___ to school yesterday.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Went waa past form-ka go. Review source: A2 Unit 1.

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Could you explain the situation clearly?
lines.somali: Xaaladda si cad ma u sharxi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Yes. I checked the details before choosing a response.
lines.somali: Haa. Faahfaahinta ayaan hubiyay ka hor inta aanan jawaab dooran.

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What happened next?

lines.english: I used the A2 language from this review section.

practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “cimilo”. Waxay ka timid Erayada aasaasiga ah ee cimilada; haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg casharkan. Review source: A2 Unit 5.

options: Qorraxdu way soo baxday kadib markii roobkii subax joogsaday.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Jobs, workplaces, duties, shifts, work at/in/for/as, routines, current work, obligations, adverts, CVs and interviews. Qaybtani waxay muujisaa strengths, weak areas iyo meesha dib loogu noqdo.
learningObjectives: Dib u eeg Jobs and Workplaces.
Aqoonso jawaabta saxda ah iyo sababteeda.
Calaamadee meelaha xooggan iyo meelaha tabarta yar.
Adeegso recommended section IDs marka practice dheeraad ahi loo baahan yahay.

grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka luqadda: Shaqooyinka iyo goobaha shaqada
grammar.explanationSomali: Jobs, workplaces, duties, shifts, work at/in/for/as, routines, current work, obligations, adverts, CVs and interviews. Xaaladda iyo macnaha ayaa go’aaminaya tense-ka, modal-ka, connector-ka ama communication function-ka.
grammar.rule: Jobs, workplaces, duties, shifts, work at/in/for/as, routines, current work, obligations, adverts, CVs and interviews.
grammar.sentenceStructure: context + accurate A2 form + useful detail + appropriate response

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Going to, arrangements, will, possibility, hopes, appointments, first conditional, time clauses and action plans. Qaybtani waxay muujisaa strengths, weak areas iyo meesha dib loogu noqdo.
learningObjectives: Dib u eeg Future Plans and Goals.
Aqoonso jawaabta saxda ah iyo sababteeda.
Calaamadee meelaha xooggan iyo meelaha tabarta yar.
Adeegso recommended section IDs marka practice dheeraad ahi loo baahan yahay.

grammar.titleEnglish: Future Plans and Goals language review
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka luqadda: Qorshayaasha iyo yoolalka mustaqbalka
grammar.explanationSomali: Going to, arrangements, will, possibility, hopes, appointments, first conditional, time clauses and action plans. Xaaladda iyo macnaha ayaa go’aaminaya tense-ka, modal-ka, connector-ka ama communication function-ka.
grammar.rule: Going to, arrangements, will, possibility, hopes, appointments, first conditional, time clauses and action plans.
grammar.sentenceStructure: context + accurate A2 form + useful detail + appropriate response

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Homes, furniture, neighbourhoods, there is/are, prepositions, directions, comparisons, too/enough, one/ones and relatives. Qaybtani waxay muujisaa strengths, weak areas iyo meesha dib loogu noqdo.
learningObjectives: Dib u eeg Places and Comparisons.
Aqoonso jawaabta saxda ah iyo sababteeda.
Calaamadee meelaha xooggan iyo meelaha tabarta yar.
Adeegso recommended section IDs marka practice dheeraad ahi loo baahan yahay.

grammar.explanationSomali: Homes, furniture, neighbourhoods, there is/are, prepositions, directions, comparisons, too/enough, one/ones and relatives. Xaaladda iyo macnaha ayaa go’aaminaya tense-ka, modal-ka, connector-ka ama communication function-ka.
grammar.rule: Homes, furniture, neighbourhoods, there is/are, prepositions, directions, comparisons, too/enough, one/ones and relatives.
grammar.sentenceStructure: context + accurate A2 form + useful detail + appropriate response

positiveExamples.english: I have already checked the fictional details.
positiveExamples.somali: Hore ayaan u hubiyay faahfaahinta tusaalaha ah.

positiveExamples.english: Could you tell me where the office is?
positiveExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa meesha xafiisku ku yaal?

positiveExamples.english: If the time changes, I will send an update.
positiveExamples.somali: Haddii waqtigu isbeddelo, cusbooneysiin ayaan diri doonaa.

positiveExamples.english: Although the route is longer, it is more convenient.
positiveExamples.somali: Inkastoo jidku dheer yahay, haddana wuu ka habboon yahay.

positiveExamples.english: I would like a replacement, please.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxaan jeclaan lahaa beddel, fadlan.

positiveExamples.english: She worked there last year.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxay halkaas ka shaqaynaysay sannadkii hore.

positiveExamples.english: They are meeting the adviser tomorrow.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxay la kulmayaan lataliyaha berri.

positiveExamples.english: You should review the recommended section.
positiveExamples.somali: Waa inaad dib u eegtaa qaybta lagu taliyay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a tense without checking the time meaning.

questionExamples.somali: Ma sharxi kartaa sababta jawaabtaasi u saxan tahay?

commonMistakesSomali: Ka dib did isticmaal base verb.
Present Perfect ha la raacin waqti hore oo dhammaaday.
Indirect question-ka ku hay statement word order.
Kala saar must not iyo do not have to.

dialogues.titleSomali: A2 Unit 10 review dialogue • listening-style • TTS-ready

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Could you explain the situation clearly?
lines.somali: Xaaladda si cad ma u sharxi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Yes. I checked the details before choosing a response.
lines.somali: Haa. Faahfaahinta ayaan hubiyay ka hor inta aanan jawaab dooran.

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What happened next?
lines.somali: Maxaa xigay?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I used the A2 language from this review section.
lines.somali: Waxaan adeegsaday luqadda A2 ee qaybtan dib-u-eegista.

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Could you explain the situation clearly?
lines.somali: Xaaladda si cad ma u sharxi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Yes. I checked the details before choosing a response.
lines.somali: Haa. Faahfaahinta ayaan hubiyay ka hor inta aanan jawaab dooran.

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What happened next?
lines.somali: Maxaa xigay?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I used the A2 language from this review section.
lines.somali: Waxaan adeegsaday luqadda A2 ee qaybtan dib-u-eegista.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Information, confirmation, problems, events, solutions, complaints, delays, forms, instructions, follow-ups and support. Qaybtani waxay muujisaa strengths, weak areas iyo meesha dib loogu noqdo.
learningObjectives: Dib u eeg Everyday Problem Solving.
Aqoonso jawaabta saxda ah iyo sababteeda.
Calaamadee meelaha xooggan iyo meelaha tabarta yar.
Adeegso recommended section IDs marka practice dheeraad ahi loo baahan yahay.

grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka luqadda: Xallinta dhibaatooyinka maalinlaha ah
grammar.explanationSomali: Information, confirmation, problems, events, solutions, complaints, delays, forms, instructions, follow-ups and support. Xaaladda iyo macnaha ayaa go’aaminaya tense-ka, modal-ka, connector-ka ama communication function-ka.
grammar.rule: Information, confirmation, problems, events, solutions, complaints, delays, forms, instructions, follow-ups and support.
grammar.sentenceStructure: context + accurate A2 form + useful detail + appropriate response

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: All major A2 tenses, modals, comparisons, quantities, verb patterns, first conditional, relatives, connectors and polite questions. Qaybtani waxay muujisaa strengths, weak areas iyo meesha dib loogu noqdo.
learningObjectives: Dib u eeg A2 Core Grammar Review.
Aqoonso jawaabta saxda ah iyo sababteeda.
Calaamadee meelaha xooggan iyo meelaha tabarta yar.
Adeegso recommended section IDs marka practice dheeraad ahi loo baahan yahay.

grammar.titleEnglish: A2 Core Grammar Review language review
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka luqadda: Dib-u-eegista grammar-ka muhiimka ah
grammar.explanationSomali: All major A2 tenses, modals, comparisons, quantities, verb patterns, first conditional, relatives, connectors and polite questions. Xaaladda iyo macnaha ayaa go’aaminaya tense-ka, modal-ka, connector-ka ama communication function-ka.
grammar.rule: All major A2 tenses, modals, comparisons, quantities, verb patterns, first conditional, relatives, connectors and polite questions.
grammar.sentenceStructure: context + accurate A2 form + useful detail + appropriate response

positiveExamples.english: I have already checked the fictional details.
positiveExamples.somali: Hore ayaan u hubiyay faahfaahinta tusaalaha ah.

positiveExamples.english: Could you tell me where the office is?
positiveExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa meesha xafiisku ku yaal?

positiveExamples.english: If the time changes, I will send an update.
positiveExamples.somali: Haddii waqtigu isbeddelo, cusbooneysiin ayaan diri doonaa.

positiveExamples.english: Although the route is longer, it is more convenient.
positiveExamples.somali: Inkastoo jidku dheer yahay, haddana wuu ka habboon yahay.

positiveExamples.english: I would like a replacement, please.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxaan jeclaan lahaa beddel, fadlan.

positiveExamples.english: She worked there last year.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxay halkaas ka shaqaynaysay sannadkii hore.

positiveExamples.english: They are meeting the adviser tomorrow.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxay la kulmayaan lataliyaha berri.

positiveExamples.english: You should review the recommended section.
positiveExamples.somali: Waa inaad dib u eegtaa qaybta lagu taliyay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a tense without checking the time meaning.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran tense adigoon hubin macnaha waqtiga.

negativeExamples.english: The learner did not use real personal information.
negativeExamples.somali: Ardaygu ma isticmaalin xog qofeed oo dhab ah.

questionExamples.english: Which form matches this situation?
questionExamples.somali: Qaabkee ayaa xaaladdan ku habboon?

questionExamples.english: Could you explain why that answer is correct?
questionExamples.somali: Ma sharxi kartaa sababta jawaabtaasi u saxan tahay?

commonMistakesSomali: Ka dib did isticmaal base verb.
Present Perfect ha la raacin waqti hore oo dhammaaday.
Indirect question-ka ku hay statement word order.
Kala saar must not iyo do not have to.

dialogues.titleSomali: A2 Unit 12 review dialogue • listening-style • TTS-ready

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Could you explain the situation clearly?
lines.somali: Xaaladda si cad ma u sharxi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Yes. I checked the details before choosing a response.
lines.somali: Haa. Faahfaahinta ayaan hubiyay ka hor inta aanan jawaab dooran.

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What happened next?
lines.somali: Maxaa xigay?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I used the A2 language from this review section.
lines.somali: Waxaan adeegsaday luqadda A2 ee qaybtan dib-u-eegista.

lessons.speakingPractice: Sharax hal xaalad oo ku saabsan A2 Core Grammar Review; isticmaal TTS-ready English phrases iyo xog mala-awaal ah.
lessons.writingPractice: Qor A2 response gaaban oo ku saabsan A2 Core Grammar Review, kadibna is-hubi.
lessons.summarySomali: Dib-u-eegista grammar-ka muhiimka ah: natiijada exercises-ka u adeegso inaad go’aamiso in qaybtan lagu celceliyo.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Two hundred and forty high-value references from Units 1–12 with topic tags, pronunciation, context and Somali meaning. Qaybtani waxay muujisaa strengths, weak areas iyo meesha dib loogu noqdo.
learningObjectives: Dib u eeg A2 Vocabulary Review.
Aqoonso jawaabta saxda ah iyo sababteeda.
Calaamadee meelaha xooggan iyo meelaha tabarta yar.
Adeegso recommended section IDs marka practice dheeraad ahi loo baahan yahay.

vocabulary.exampleEnglish: Amina said, “Good morning” while meeting someone at a course, and her partner responded appropriately.
vocabulary.exampleSomali: Intii lagu jiray tababbarka, Aamina waxay tiri “Good morning” si ay u muujiso “subax wanaagsan”, qofkii kalena si ku habboon ayuu uga jawaabay.
vocabulary.explanationSomali: Function: greeting. Tone: neutral. Weedhan waxaa loo adeegsadaa subax wanaagsan iyadoo la eegayo qofka iyo xaaladda. Review source: A2 Unit 11.
vocabulary.commonMistakeSomali: Dooro tone-ka ku habboon, isticmaal collocation-ka saxda ah, kana jawaab si waafaqsan context-ka.

grammar.explanationSomali: Two hundred and forty high-value references from Units 1–12 with topic tags, pronunciation, context and Somali meaning. Xaaladda iyo macnaha ayaa go’aaminaya tense-ka, modal-ka, connector-ka ama communication function-ka.
grammar.rule: Two hundred and forty high-value references from Units 1–12 with topic tags, pronunciation, context and Somali meaning.
grammar.sentenceStructure: context + accurate A2 form + useful detail + appropriate response

positiveExamples.english: I have already checked the fictional details.
positiveExamples.somali: Hore ayaan u hubiyay faahfaahinta tusaalaha ah.

positiveExamples.english: Could you tell me where the office is?
positiveExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa meesha xafiisku ku yaal?

positiveExamples.english: If the time changes, I will send an update.
positiveExamples.somali: Haddii waqtigu isbeddelo, cusbooneysiin ayaan diri doonaa.

positiveExamples.english: Although the route is longer, it is more convenient.
positiveExamples.somali: Inkastoo jidku dheer yahay, haddana wuu ka habboon yahay.

positiveExamples.english: I would like a replacement, please.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxaan jeclaan lahaa beddel, fadlan.

positiveExamples.english: She worked there last year.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxay halkaas ka shaqaynaysay sannadkii hore.

positiveExamples.english: They are meeting the adviser tomorrow.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxay la kulmayaan lataliyaha berri.

positiveExamples.english: You should review the recommended section.
positiveExamples.somali: Waa inaad dib u eegtaa qaybta lagu taliyay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a tense without checking the time meaning.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran tense adigoon hubin macnaha waqtiga.

negativeExamples.english: The learner did not use real personal information.
negativeExamples.somali: Ardaygu ma isticmaalin xog qofeed oo dhab ah.

questionExamples.english: Which form matches this situation?
questionExamples.somali: Qaabkee ayaa xaaladdan ku habboon?

questionExamples.english: Could you explain why that answer is correct?
questionExamples.somali: Ma sharxi kartaa sababta jawaabtaasi u saxan tahay?

commonMistakesSomali: Ka dib did isticmaal base verb.
Present Perfect ha la raacin waqti hore oo dhammaaday.
Indirect question-ka ku hay statement word order.
Kala saar must not iyo do not have to.

dialogues.titleSomali: A2 Unit 12 review dialogue • listening-style • TTS-ready

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: Could you explain the situation clearly?
lines.somali: Xaaladda si cad ma u sharxi kartaa?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: Yes. I checked the details before choosing a response.
lines.somali: Haa. Faahfaahinta ayaan hubiyay ka hor inta aanan jawaab dooran.

lines.speaker: Amina
lines.english: What happened next?
lines.somali: Maxaa xigay?

lines.speaker: Hassan
lines.english: I used the A2 language from this review section.
lines.somali: Waxaan adeegsaday luqadda A2 ee qaybtan dib-u-eegista.

Calaamadee meelaha xooggan iyo meelaha tabarta yar.
Adeegso recommended section IDs marka practice dheeraad ahi loo baahan yahay.

dialogues.titleSomali: Reading 1 • 180–320 words • 10 questions

lines.speaker: Reader
lines.english:
Last spring, Yusuf joined a community clean-up near a public park. He had never taken part in a large local event before, so he felt nervous when he arrived. First, the organizer welcomed the volunteers and explained the plan. Yusuf and three other volunteers collected paper and plastic beside the walking path, while another group cleaned the children’s area.

At midday, dark clouds appeared and heavy rain began suddenly. The volunteers moved the equipment into a small building and waited. Yusuf used the break to speak with people from another neighbourhood. They shared food, discussed their work, and compared ideas for future events.

After thirty minutes, the rain stopped. The paths were wet, but the group decided to finish the most important tasks. Yusuf helped carry full bags to the collection point and checked that no tools were missing. Finally, the organizer thanked everyone and took a group photograph.

Yusuf was tired when he returned home, but he was proud of what the team had achieved. The experience taught him that a simple local activity could help the environment and introduce him to new people. He later wrote a short message to the organizer and offered to help at the next event.
lines.somali: Qoraalkan cusub wuxuu dib u eegayaa fahamka guud, faahfaahinta, isku xigxiga, vocabulary context, grammar iyo inference fudud.

dialogues.titleSomali: Reading 2 • 180–320 words • 10 questions

lines.speaker: Reader
lines.english:
Amina is planning a fictional journey from Harbour Town to Green Valley next month. She compared three travel options before making a decision. The express bus was the cheapest, but it left at six in the morning. The train was more comfortable and had space for luggage, although its ticket cost twenty dollars more. A shared car was the fastest option, but the collection point was far from her home.

Amina chose the train because she could reach the station by a short local bus ride. She booked a morning ticket and received a confirmation showing platform four. Two days later, the transport company sent an update: engineering work would delay the train by forty minutes and move it to platform six.

She checked the new departure time and contacted her hotel. The receptionist said that late check-in would not be a problem, but asked her to send a message after arrival. Amina added the hotel address and reception number to her offline notes.

On the travel day, she plans to leave home early, carry water and a light meal, and check the departure board before going to the platform. If the delay becomes longer, she will use the station help desk to ask about another connection. Her careful preparation means that one schedule change should not ruin the journey.
lines.somali: Qoraalkan cusub wuxuu dib u eegayaa fahamka guud, faahfaahinta, isku xigxiga, vocabulary context, grammar iyo inference fudud.

dialogues.titleSomali: Reading 3 • 180–320 words • 10 questions

lines.speaker: Reader
lines.english:
Hassan works as an office assistant for a fictional training centre. On Monday morning, his manager asked him to prepare a room for an afternoon workshop. Hassan checked the booking, arranged twenty chairs, tested the projector and placed attendance forms near the entrance.

At eleven, another employee said that the workshop had moved to a larger room. Hassan looked at the shared schedule, but it still showed the original room. Instead of moving everything immediately, he called the manager and asked for confirmation. The manager explained that a customer meeting would use the first room, so the workshop really had changed.

Hassan asked two colleagues for help. They carried the materials together and finished before lunch. While Hassan was connecting the laptop, the projector stopped working. He reported the problem to technical support and requested another piece of equipment. A technician arrived twenty minutes later and replaced the cable.

The participants found the new room without difficulty because Hassan put a clear notice at reception and sent a short update. After the workshop, his manager thanked the team for responding calmly. Hassan recorded the equipment problem and the room change in the daily report. The experience showed that checking information first can prevent unnecessary work and confusion.
lines.somali: Qoraalkan cusub wuxuu dib u eegayaa fahamka guud, faahfaahinta, isku xigxiga, vocabulary context, grammar iyo inference fudud.

dialogues.titleSomali: Reading 4 • 180–320 words • 10 questions

lines.speaker: Reader
lines.english:
When Hodan began an evening English course, she understood simple conversations but found longer reading tasks difficult. During the first month, she tried to study for two hours every night. This plan was too demanding, and she often stopped after a few days.

Her teacher suggested a shorter weekly routine. Hodan started reading for twenty minutes on four evenings and reviewing vocabulary on the bus. She also joined a small study group on Saturday. The group members did not correct every small mistake; instead, they focused on whether each message was clear.

After six weeks, Hodan had finished three short books and had written eight practice paragraphs. She could describe past events more confidently and had already learned how to ask polite indirect questions. However, she still confused the Past Simple and Present Perfect when a sentence did not include a clear time expression.

Hodan checked her quiz results and made a list of weak topics. She decided to review tense examples before starting new material. She also recorded useful phrases and listened to them with text-to-speech. Her progress was not immediate, but the regular routine worked better than occasional long study sessions. She now plans to complete the A2 review before attempting the final assessment.
lines.somali: Qoraalkan cusub wuxuu dib u eegayaa fahamka guud, faahfaahinta, isku xigxiga, vocabulary context, grammar iyo inference fudud.

dialogues.titleSomali: Reading 5 • 180–320 words • 10 questions

lines.speaker: Reader
lines.english:
Riverside is a fictional neighbourhood on the edge of a medium-sized city. Ten years ago, the area had only a few shops and one bus route. It has changed considerably since then. There is now a health clinic, a public library, two supermarkets and a small sports centre.

The library is opposite the clinic and next to a quiet public garden. Behind the garden, a walking path follows the river for two kilometres. The path is popular in the early evening, although it becomes muddy after heavy rain. The busiest place is Market Square, where local businesses sell food, clothes and household items.

Riverside is more convenient than it was in the past, but residents still mention two disadvantages. Traffic is heavier near the square, and there are not enough parking spaces. The city is going to add a second bus route next year, which may reduce the number of cars.

For a student without a car, the best place to live would be near the library because the bus stop, shops and study spaces are within walking distance. A family that wants a quieter street might prefer the western side. Riverside is not the cheapest neighbourhood in the city, but its services and green spaces make it an attractive option.
lines.somali: Qoraalkan cusub wuxuu dib u eegayaa fahamka guud, faahfaahinta, isku xigxiga, vocabulary context, grammar iyo inference fudud.

dialogues.titleSomali: Reading 6 • 180–320 words • 10 questions

lines.speaker: Reader
lines.english:
On Wednesday, Maryan received an invitation to a small workplace gathering on Friday evening. Her colleague, Abdi, was leaving the company after three years, and the team planned to meet at a local restaurant. Maryan wanted to attend, but she had already arranged an online lesson at the same time.

She did not ignore the invitation. She thanked the organizer and explained that she might arrive late because of the lesson. Then she asked whether the group would still be at the restaurant after eight. The organizer replied that dinner would begin at seven thirty and that people would probably stay until ten.

Maryan accepted the invitation and said she would join after class. On Friday afternoon, her internet service became unavailable, so the teacher postponed the lesson. Maryan sent another message to confirm that she could now arrive on time.

At the gathering, she met two colleagues from another department. She began with small talk about the restaurant and asked how they had worked with Abdi. She listened carefully and used follow-up questions instead of changing the subject quickly. Before leaving, she thanked the organizer and said goodbye to the group. The evening showed how clear updates, polite questions and active listening can make social arrangements easier.
lines.somali: Qoraalkan cusub wuxuu dib u eegayaa fahamka guud, faahfaahinta, isku xigxiga, vocabulary context, grammar iyo inference fudud.

dialogues.titleSomali: Reading 7 • 180–320 words • 10 questions

lines.speaker: Reader
lines.english:
A fictional customer ordered a desk lamp from an online shop. The confirmation described a black lamp with an adjustable arm, but the package contained a white lamp with a fixed arm. The item worked, yet it was different from the product the customer had selected.

First, the customer checked the receipt and order reference. Then she used the shop’s support chat and wrote a clear description of the problem. The agent initially thought she wanted to return a faulty lamp. The customer clarified that the lamp was not faulty; it was the wrong model.

The agent apologized for the misunderstanding and offered two options. The shop could send the correct lamp within four working days, or it could provide a refund after the incorrect item was returned. The customer needed a lamp for a study desk before the weekend, so a later replacement was not suitable.

She politely asked whether she could collect the correct model from a fictional local branch. The agent checked the stock and confirmed that one was available. The customer received a collection reference and returned the white lamp the next morning. The branch checked the receipt and completed the exchange. The customer later sent a short message confirming that the issue had been resolved. Clear details helped both sides find a practical solution.
lines.somali: Qoraalkan cusub wuxuu dib u eegayaa fahamka guud, faahfaahinta, isku xigxiga, vocabulary context, grammar iyo inference fudud.

dialogues.titleSomali: Reading 8 • 180–320 words • 10 questions

lines.speaker: Reader
lines.english:
Ahmed wants to improve his English so that he can communicate more confidently with customers and continue his education. He has created a six-month action plan instead of setting one general goal. During the first two months, he is going to review A2 grammar and practise short conversations three times a week.

In the third month, he plans to write one work-related message every weekday. Some messages will request information, while others will report a simple problem or confirm an arrangement. A colleague has agreed to read them and point out unclear sentences.

Ahmed may join an online speaking group in the fourth month. He has not chosen a group yet because he wants to compare schedules and class sizes. If he finds a suitable evening group, he will attend for at least eight weeks.

During the final two months, Ahmed will prepare for a more advanced course. He will continue reviewing weak areas instead of stopping A2 practice completely. His main challenge is time: his work schedule sometimes changes at short notice. For that reason, he has included shorter activities that he can complete on busy days.

Ahmed knows that progress will not be perfect every week. However, the plan gives him clear actions, flexible options and a way to measure improvement. At the end of six months, he will review his results and decide on the next goal.
lines.somali: Qoraalkan cusub wuxuu dib u eegayaa fahamka guud, faahfaahinta, isku xigxiga, vocabulary context, grammar iyo inference fudud.

lessons.shortDescriptionSomali: Fifteen guided tasks covering messages, paragraphs, comparisons, apologies, requests, follow-ups and problem reports. Qaybtani waxay muujisaa strengths, weak areas iyo meesha dib loogu noqdo.
learningObjectives: Dib u eeg Writing and Communication Review.
Aqoonso jawaabta saxda ah iyo sababteeda.
Calaamadee meelaha xooggan iyo meelaha tabarta yar.
Adeegso recommended section IDs marka practice dheeraad ahi loo baahan yahay.

grammar.titleEnglish: Writing and Communication Review language review
grammar.titleSomali: Qaabka luqadda: Dib-u-eegista qoraalka iyo xiriirka
grammar.explanationSomali: Fifteen guided tasks covering messages, paragraphs, comparisons, apologies, requests, follow-ups and problem reports. Xaaladda iyo macnaha ayaa go’aaminaya tense-ka, modal-ka, connector-ka ama communication function-ka.
grammar.rule: Fifteen guided tasks covering messages, paragraphs, comparisons, apologies, requests, follow-ups and problem reports.
grammar.sentenceStructure: context + accurate A2 form + useful detail + appropriate response

positiveExamples.english: I have already checked the fictional details.
positiveExamples.somali: Hore ayaan u hubiyay faahfaahinta tusaalaha ah.

positiveExamples.english: Could you tell me where the office is?
positiveExamples.somali: Ma ii sheegi kartaa meesha xafiisku ku yaal?

positiveExamples.english: If the time changes, I will send an update.
positiveExamples.somali: Haddii waqtigu isbeddelo, cusbooneysiin ayaan diri doonaa.

positiveExamples.english: Although the route is longer, it is more convenient.
positiveExamples.somali: Inkastoo jidku dheer yahay, haddana wuu ka habboon yahay.

positiveExamples.english: I would like a replacement, please.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxaan jeclaan lahaa beddel, fadlan.

positiveExamples.english: She worked there last year.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxay halkaas ka shaqaynaysay sannadkii hore.

positiveExamples.english: They are meeting the adviser tomorrow.
positiveExamples.somali: Waxay la kulmayaan lataliyaha berri.

positiveExamples.english: You should review the recommended section.
positiveExamples.somali: Waa inaad dib u eegtaa qaybta lagu taliyay.

negativeExamples.english: Do not choose a tense without checking the time meaning.
negativeExamples.somali: Ha dooran tense adigoon hubin macnaha waqtiga.

negativeExamples.english: The learner did not use real personal information.

commonMistakesSomali: Ka dib did isticmaal base verb.
Present Perfect ha la raacin waqti hore oo dhammaaday.
Indirect question-ka ku hay statement word order.
Kala saar must not iyo do not have to.

dialogues.titleSomali: Past-event paragraph • suggested 120 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a past-event paragraph using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Past-event paragraph adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Last Saturday, I joined a fictional community event. First, the organizer explained our tasks. I worked with three volunteers, and we prepared a reading area for children. Later, rain interrupted the activity, so we moved the books inside. When the weather improved, we finished the work and spoke with local families. I was tired at the end, but I felt proud because the team completed the plan. The experience taught me to ask questions when instructions are unclear and to help other people when a task becomes difficult.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Travel message • suggested 80 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a travel message using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Travel message adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Hello Amina. My train has been delayed by forty minutes and the platform has changed from four to six. I should arrive at Green Valley at about three fifteen. Could you please tell the hotel that I will check in later than planned? I will send another message after I arrive. Thank you.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Appointment message • suggested 70 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a appointment message using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Appointment message adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Good morning. I have an appointment on Thursday at eleven, but my work schedule has changed. Could I move it to Friday afternoon? I am available after two o’clock. Please let me know whether another time is possible. Thank you for your help.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Product complaint • suggested 150 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a product complaint using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Product complaint adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Hello Service Team. I am writing about a fictional order that arrived yesterday. I ordered a medium blue shirt, but the package contained a small black shirt. The item does not match the order confirmation, and I need the correct shirt before Friday. I would like to exchange it for the product shown on the receipt. Could you explain the return steps and confirm whether the medium blue shirt is available? If it is unavailable, I would prefer a refund. I have kept the mock receipt and reference. Thank you for checking the problem.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Weather report • suggested 110 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a weather report using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Weather report adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Today is warm and cloudy, and the temperature is twenty-four degrees. Light rain is falling near the coast, while inland areas are staying dry. Tomorrow will probably be cooler, and strong winds may arrive in the afternoon. People who are travelling should check local updates before leaving. The weekend is going to begin with rain, but Sunday may be brighter and warmer.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Job application message • suggested 130 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a job application message using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Job application message adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Dear Hiring Manager. I am writing to apply for the fictional office-assistant position. I have experience helping customers, organizing documents and preparing meeting rooms. I can use common computer programs and communicate in Somali and English. In my previous mock role, I answered messages and checked weekly schedules. I am reliable, organized and comfortable working with a team. I would be happy to attend an interview and explain my experience in more detail. Please find my practice CV attached. Kind regards, Amina.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Learning-progress paragraph • suggested 120 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a learning-progress paragraph using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Learning-progress paragraph adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: I have studied English regularly for six months. At first, I found past-tense verbs and longer reading passages difficult. I started using a weekly plan and have already completed several short books. I also practise speaking with a classmate every Saturday. My vocabulary has improved, but I still need to review indirect questions and Present Perfect forms. Before the final exam, I am going to repeat the grammar section and write two practice paragraphs each week.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Invitation • suggested 70 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a invitation using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Invitation adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Hi Hassan. We are having a small fictional study-group meeting on Saturday at three. We are going to review travel vocabulary and practise short dialogues. Would you like to join us online? Please let me know by Friday. If Saturday is difficult, we could meet next week instead.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Future-goal paragraph • suggested 130 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a future-goal paragraph using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Future-goal paragraph adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: My goal is to communicate more confidently at work. During the next three months, I am going to practise customer conversations and write short professional messages. I may join an evening course if the schedule is suitable. I will also review my mistakes every Friday. If I complete these activities regularly, I will be better prepared for new responsibilities. I know that some weeks will be busy; however, I can use shorter exercises on those days.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Place description • suggested 130 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a place description using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Place description adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Harbour View is a fictional neighbourhood near the coast. There is a busy market in the centre and a quiet walking path beside the water. The library, clinic and main bus stop are close to Market Square. The eastern side is more convenient, but it is noisier than the western streets. For students, the area near the library is the best option because study spaces and transport are nearby. Although parking is limited, most daily services are within walking distance.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Apology message • suggested 65 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a apology message using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Apology message adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Hello Fatima. I am sorry that I missed our fictional meeting this morning. I wrote the wrong time in my calendar and did not see your reminder. It was my mistake. Could we arrange another time on Thursday? I will confirm the new appointment the day before. Kind regards, Hodan.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Information request • suggested 70 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a information request using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Information request adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Good afternoon. I would like more information about the fictional evening computer course. Could you tell me when the next course begins, how long it lasts and whether online classes are available? I would also like to know the weekly schedule. Thank you for your assistance.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Follow-up message • suggested 65 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a follow-up message using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Follow-up message adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Hello. I am following up on the fictional replacement request I sent on Monday. I have not received a confirmation yet. Could you please provide an update and tell me whether the correct item is available? The mock reference is U12-204. Thank you for looking into this.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Comparison paragraph • suggested 125 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a comparison paragraph using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Comparison paragraph adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Property A is cheaper than Property B, and it is closer to public transport. However, Property B is larger and has the most comfortable study area. The two properties are equally close to shops, but Property A is not as quiet as Property B. For a student with a limited budget, Property A is the better choice. A family that needs more space might prefer Property B even though its monthly rent is higher.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

dialogues.titleSomali: Integrated everyday problem report • suggested 180 words • prompt, key points, useful phrases iyo checklist

lines.speaker: Prompt
lines.english: Write a integrated everyday problem report using clear A2 grammar, the suggested length and fictional details.
lines.somali: Qor Integrated everyday problem report adigoo isticmaalaya grammar A2, dhererka la soo jeediyay iyo xog mala-awaal ah.

lines.speaker: Model
lines.english: Hello Support Team. I am writing to report a problem with a fictional course booking. I registered for the Tuesday evening class and received reference A2-315. Yesterday, a confirmation message showed Thursday morning instead. I contacted the information desk, but the employee could only see the Thursday booking. I may not have explained the original request clearly, so I have checked my practice form again. It shows Tuesday at six thirty. I work on Thursday mornings and cannot attend that session. Could you please check the registration record and move the booking to Tuesday evening? If that class is full, Wednesday evening would also be suitable. I would appreciate a written confirmation showing the final day, time and room. I have not included any real account or contact information because this is a language-learning exercise. Thank you for reviewing the details and explaining the available options.
lines.somali: Model-ku wuxuu muujinayaa opening, faahfaahin muhiim ah, isku-xirnaan iyo closing ku habboon.

practiceExercises.question: Write a appointment message of about 70 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Good morning. I have an appointment on Thursday at eleven, but my work schedule has changed. Could I move it to Friday afternoon? I am available after two o’clock. Please let me know whether another time is possible. Thank you for your help.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Good morning. I have an appointment on Thursday at eleven, but my work schedule has changed. Could I move it to Friday afternoon? I am available after two o’clock. Please let me know whether another time is possible. Thank you for your help.”. Source: A2 guided writing task 3. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg qaybta lagu taliyay.

practiceExercises.question: Write a product complaint of about 150 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Hello Service Team. I am writing about a fictional order that arrived yesterday. I ordered a medium blue shirt, but the package contained a small black shirt. The item does not match the order confirmation, and I need the correct shirt before Friday. I would like to exchange it for the product shown on the receipt. Could you explain the return steps and confirm whether the medium blue shirt is available? If it is unavailable, I would prefer a refund. I have kept the mock receipt and reference. Thank you for checking the problem.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Hello Service Team. I am writing about a fictional order that arrived yesterday. I ordered a medium blue shirt, but the package contained a small black shirt. The item does not match the order confirmation, and I need the correct shirt before Friday. I would like to exchange it for the product shown on the receipt. Could you explain the return steps and confirm whether the medium blue shirt is available? If it is unavailable, I would prefer a refund. I have kept the mock receipt and reference. Thank you for checking the problem.”. Source: A2 guided writing task 4. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg qaybta lagu taliyay.

practiceExercises.question: Write a weather report of about 110 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Today is warm and cloudy, and the temperature is twenty-four degrees. Light rain is falling near the coast, while inland areas are staying dry. Tomorrow will probably be cooler, and strong winds may arrive in the afternoon. People who are travelling should check local updates before leaving. The weekend is going to begin with rain, but Sunday may be brighter and warmer.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Today is warm and cloudy, and the temperature is twenty-four degrees. Light rain is falling near the coast, while inland areas are staying dry. Tomorrow will probably be cooler, and strong winds may arrive in the afternoon. People who are travelling should check local updates before leaving. The weekend is going to begin with rain, but Sunday may be brighter and warmer.”. Source: A2 guided writing task 5. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg qaybta lagu taliyay.

practiceExercises.question: Write a job application message of about 130 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Dear Hiring Manager. I am writing to apply for the fictional office-assistant position. I have experience helping customers, organizing documents and preparing meeting rooms. I can use common computer programs and communicate in Somali and English. In my previous mock role, I answered messages and checked weekly schedules. I am reliable, organized and comfortable working with a team. I would be happy to attend an interview and explain my experience in more detail. Please find my practice CV attached. Kind regards, Amina.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Dear Hiring Manager. I am writing to apply for the fictional office-assistant position. I have experience helping customers, organizing documents and preparing meeting rooms. I can use common computer programs and communicate in Somali and English. In my previous mock role, I answered messages and checked weekly schedules. I am reliable, organized and comfortable working with a team. I would be happy to attend an interview and explain my experience in more detail. Please find my practice CV attached. Kind regards, Amina.”. Source: A2 guided writing task 6. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg qaybta lagu taliyay.

practiceExercises.question: Write a learning-progress paragraph of about 120 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: I have studied English regularly for six months. At first, I found past-tense verbs and longer reading passages difficult. I started using a weekly plan and have already completed several short books. I also practise speaking with a classmate every Saturday. My vocabulary has improved, but I still need to review indirect questions and Present Perfect forms. Before the final exam, I am going to repeat the grammar section and write two practice paragraphs each week.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “I have studied English regularly for six months. At first, I found past-tense verbs and longer reading passages difficult. I started using a weekly plan and have already completed several short books. I also practise speaking with a classmate every Saturday. My vocabulary has improved, but I still need to review indirect questions and Present Perfect forms. Before the final exam, I am going to repeat the grammar section and write two practice paragraphs each week.”. Source: A2 guided writing task 7. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg qaybta lagu taliyay.

practiceExercises.question: Write a invitation of about 70 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Hi Hassan. We are having a small fictional study-group meeting on Saturday at three. We are going to review travel vocabulary and practise short dialogues. Would you like to join us online? Please let me know by Friday. If Saturday is difficult, we could meet next week instead.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Hi Hassan. We are having a small fictional study-group meeting on Saturday at three. We are going to review travel vocabulary and practise short dialogues. Would you like to join us online? Please let me know by Friday. If Saturday is difficult, we could meet next week instead.”. practiceExercises.question: Write a future-goal paragraph of about 130 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: My goal is to communicate more confidently at work. During the next three months, I am going to practise customer conversations and write short professional messages. I may join an evening course if the schedule is suitable. I will also review my mistakes every Friday. If I complete these activities regularly, I will be better prepared for new responsibilities. I know that some weeks will be busy; however, I can use shorter exercises on those days.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “My goal is to communicate more confidently at work. During the next three months, I am going to practise customer conversations and write short professional messages. I may join an evening course if the schedule is suitable. I will also review my mistakes every Friday. If I complete these activities regularly, I will be better prepared for new responsibilities. I know that some weeks will be busy; however, I can use shorter exercises on those days.”. Source: A2 guided writing task 9. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg qaybta lagu taliyay.

practiceExercises.question: Write a place description of about 130 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Harbour View is a fictional neighbourhood near the coast. There is a busy market in the centre and a quiet walking path beside the water. The library, clinic and main bus stop are close to Market Square. The eastern side is more convenient, but it is noisier than the western streets. For students, the area near the library is the best option because study spaces and transport are nearby. Although parking is limited, most daily services are within walking distance.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Harbour View is a fictional neighbourhood near the coast. There is a busy market in the centre and a quiet walking path beside the water. The library, clinic and main bus stop are close to Market Square. The eastern side is more convenient, but it is noisier than the western streets. For students, the area near the library is the best option because study spaces and transport are nearby. Although parking is limited, most daily services are within walking distance.”. Source: A2 guided writing task 10. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg qaybta lagu taliyay.

practiceExercises.question: Write a apology message of about 65 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Hello Fatima. I am sorry that I missed our fictional meeting this morning. I wrote the wrong time in my calendar and did not see your reminder. It was my mistake. Could we arrange another time on Thursday? I will confirm the new appointment the day before. Kind regards, Hodan.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Hello Fatima. I am sorry that I missed our fictional meeting this morning. I wrote the wrong time in my calendar and did not see your reminder. It was my mistake. Could we arrange another time on Thursday? I will confirm the new appointment the day before. Kind regards, Hodan.”. Source: A2 guided writing task 11. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg qaybta lagu taliyay.

practiceExercises.question: Write a information request of about 70 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Good afternoon. I would like more information about the fictional evening computer course. Could you tell me when the next course begins, how long it lasts and whether online classes are available? I would also like to know the weekly schedule. Thank you for your assistance.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Good afternoon. I would like more information about the fictional evening computer course. practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Hello. I am following up on the fictional replacement request I sent on Monday. I have not received a confirmation yet. Could you please provide an update and tell me whether the correct item is available? The mock reference is U12-204. Thank you for looking into this.

practiceExercises.question: Write a comparison paragraph of about 125 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Property A is cheaper than Property B, and it is closer to public transport. However, Property B is larger and has the most comfortable study area. The two properties are equally close to shops, but Property A is not as quiet as Property B. For a student with a limited budget, Property A is the better choice. A family that needs more space might prefer Property B even though its monthly rent is higher.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Property A is cheaper than Property B, and it is closer to public transport. However, Property B is larger and has the most comfortable study area. The two properties are equally close to shops, but Property A is not as quiet as Property B. For a student with a limited budget, Property A is the better choice. A family that needs more space might prefer Property B even though its monthly rent is higher.”. Source: A2 guided writing task 14. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg qaybta lagu taliyay.

practiceExercises.question: Write a integrated everyday problem report of about 180 words. Use fictional details, a clear opening, key information, connectors and an appropriate closing.
practiceExercises.correctAnswer: Hello Support Team. I am writing to report a problem with a fictional course booking. I registered for the Tuesday evening class and received reference A2-315. Yesterday, a confirmation message showed Thursday morning instead. I contacted the information desk, but the employee could only see the Thursday booking. I may not have explained the original request clearly, so I have checked my practice form again. It shows Tuesday at six thirty. I work on Thursday mornings and cannot attend that session. Could you please check the registration record and move the booking to Tuesday evening? If that class is full, Wednesday evening would also be suitable. I would appreciate a written confirmation showing the final day, time and room. I have not included any real account or contact information because this is a language-learning exercise. Thank you for reviewing the details and explaining the available options.
practiceExercises.explanationSomali: Jawaabta saxda ahi waa “Hello Support Team. I am writing to report a problem with a fictional course booking. I registered for the Tuesday evening class and received reference A2-315. Yesterday, a confirmation message showed Thursday morning instead. I contacted the information desk, but the employee could only see the Thursday booking. I may not have explained the original request clearly, so I have checked my practice form again. It shows Tuesday at six thirty. I work on Thursday mornings and cannot attend that session. Could you please check the registration record and move the booking to Tuesday evening? If that class is full, Wednesday evening would also be suitable. I would appreciate a written confirmation showing the final day, time and room. I have not included any real account or contact information because this is a language-learning exercise. Thank you for reviewing the details and explaining the available options.”. Source: A2 guided writing task 15. Haddii aad qaladdo, dib u eeg qaybta lagu taliyay.
